title: lepidaploa pari
intro: Lepidaploa pari, a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, is also known as Parijat or Kadamba. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Lepidaploa pari include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, digestive disorders, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant are used in teas, infusions, and decoctions to leverage its medicinal properties.
